questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Acrimonious
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Amicable
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Bitter
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Hostile
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sharp
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate antonym of "acrimonious" is "amicable." Explanation: • Acrimonious means angry, bitter, or filled with harsh feelings, especially in a dispute or argument. • Amicable means friendly, peaceful, or showing goodwill, which is the opposite of being bitter or hostile.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. Talented singers are__________, but true superstars who captivate audiences are rare.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} The cream of the crop
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A rare cat
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A dime a dozen
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In a league of their own
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct idiom to fill in the blank is "A dime a dozen." Explanation: • "A dime a dozen" means something that is very common and easy to find. In the context of the sentence, it suggests that talented singers are common or abundant, but true superstars who captivate audiences are rare. So, the complete sentence would be: "Talented singers are a dime a dozen, but true superstars who captivate audiences are rare."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. She was known for her GENEROSITY, but her brother was infamous for his stinginess.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Kindness
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Charity
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Selflessness
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Greed
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "Generosity" is "Greed" Explanation: • Generosity involves a willingness to give and share with others, often without expecting anything in return. It reflects a selfless attitude toward helping others. • Greed, on the other hand, is an intense desire to accumulate more than what is necessary, often without concern for others' needs. It involves hoarding or taking more for oneself, which is the opposite of sharing or giving.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Arriving late /the student /slipped into the room /where everyone stared at them.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} the student
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} slipped into the room
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} where everyone stared at them
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Arriving late
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct alternative is "where everyone stared at them". Explanation: • Since "the student" is singular, the pronoun must match in number. Therefore, "them" should be replaced with "him" or "her", depending on the student's gender. • If the gender of the student is not specified, you can use "his/her" as a gender-neutral alternative, or simply "their" if you prefer a gender-neutral option. So, the sentence would read correctly as: "Arriving late, the student slipped into the room where everyone stared at him/her."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the bold sentence. A remedy for all diseases
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Panacea
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Elixir
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Antidote
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Placebo
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct one-word substitute for "A remedy for all diseases" is "Panacea" Explanation: • Panacea refers to a solution or remedy for all problems or diseases, making it the most accurate one-word substitute. • Elixir is typically a medicinal solution, but it doesn’t imply a cure for all diseases like "panacea." • Antidote refers specifically to a remedy for poison, not for all diseases. • Placebo refers to a substance that has no therapeutic effect but is given to satisfy a patient's belief in treatment. Thus, "Panacea" is the best choice.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. During the meeting /every employee were asked /to share their ideas, /which improved teamwork.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} to share their ideas,
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} every employee were asked
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} During the meeting
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} which improved teamwork
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The part of the sentence that contains an error is "every employee were asked." Explanation: • "Every" is a singular subject, so it should be followed by a singular verb. The correct verb form is "was", not "were". The correct sentence should be: "During the meeting, every employee was asked to share their ideas, which improved teamwork."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. “Wow! What a beautiful painting!” she said.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} She exclaimed with astonishment that it was a beautiful painting.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She said that it was a beautiful painting.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She wondered if it was a beautiful painting.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She exclaimed with admiration that it was a beautiful painting.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is "She exclaimed with admiration that it was a beautiful painting." Explanation: • In indirect speech, the exclamation "Wow! What a beautiful painting!" is expressing a strong emotion or admiration. The phrase "exclaimed with admiration" is appropriate to convey the excitement and admiration in indirect speech. • The sentence "She said that it was a beautiful painting" changes the exclamation into a simple statement and does not accurately capture the emotional tone of the original. • "She wondered if it was a beautiful painting" implies doubt or uncertainty, which is not in line with the enthusiastic exclamation.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the sentence that has a spelling error.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} The teacher emphasized the importance of punctuality and discipline.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The school library has a vast collection of books on various subjects.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The sentence that has a spelling error is: "The principal's speech was filled with insperational messages." Explanation: The word "insperational" is incorrectly spelled. The correct spelling is "inspirational".

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the sentence that has a grammatical error.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} The movie we watched last night was thrilling.
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She doesn’t know where is the key.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} He always arrives on time for meetings.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} After running, I felt exhausted but happy.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The sentence that contains a grammatical error is: "She doesn’t know where is the key." Explanation: • In reported speech or indirect questions, the word order should be subject + verb, not verb + subject. • The correct sentence should be: "She doesn’t know where the key is."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The mechanic fixes the car whenever it breaks down.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} The car was fixed whenever it breaks down by the mechanic.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The car is being fixed by the mechanic whenever it breaks down.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The car is fixed by the mechanic whenever it breaks down.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The car fixes by the mechanic whenever it breaks down.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is "The car is fixed by the mechanic whenever it breaks down." Explanation: • The original sentence is in active voice, where "the mechanic" is performing the action of fixing the car. • In passive voice, the object of the action (the car) becomes the subject, and the focus is shifted to the car rather than the mechanic. The verb is changed to the passive form: "is fixed".

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The chef prepares a delicious meal every evening.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} A delicious meal was prepared by the chef every evening.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A delicious meal is being prepared every evening by the chef.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A delicious meal prepares by the chef every evening.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A delicious meal is prepared by the chef every evening.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is "A delicious meal is prepared by the chef every evening." Explanation: • The original sentence is in active voice, where the subject (the chef) performs the action of preparing the meal. • The passive voice version of this sentence is "A delicious meal is prepared by the chef every evening." In passive voice, the object (the meal) becomes the subject of the sentence, and the action is still done by the chef, but now the focus is on the meal rather than the chef.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Rahul told me that he would finish the project by next day.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Rahul told me, “He will finish the project by tomorrow.”

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul said to me, “I would finish the project by tomorrow.”

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul told me, “I finish the project by tomorrow.”

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul said to me, “I will finish the project by tomorrow.”
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is "Rahul said to me, “I will finish the project by tomorrow.”" Explanation: In direct speech, the exact words spoken by a person are quoted directly, without any changes. The pronoun and tense are adjusted to reflect what was actually said. The original sentence is "Rahul told me that he would finish the project by next day." In direct speech, it would become "Rahul said to me, 'I will finish the project by tomorrow.'" Here, "he" becomes "I" (since Rahul is speaking) and the verb tense shifts from "would" to "will" (to maintain the direct speech present tense format).

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The novel's _______ plot kept readers hooked until the very last page.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Greedy
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Gripping
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Grating
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Grumpy
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is "Gripping." Explanation: • Gripping means something that holds attention tightly, which fits well with the context of a novel that keeps readers hooked until the last page. • Greedy refers to an excessive desire for something, usually money or possessions, and doesn't fit the context of a plot. • Grating means something harsh or unpleasant, which would not describe a plot that keeps readers engaged. • Grumpy refers to being irritable or in a bad mood, which doesn't fit the context of a novel's plot. Thus, "Gripping" is the best choice, as it describes a plot that is engaging and captivating.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. 1. After a few minutes, the train pulled into the station, and I stepped out onto the platform. 2. I boarded the train early in the morning, just as the sun was rising. 3. The scenery outside was breathtaking, with rolling hills and picturesque villages passing by. 4. As the train chugged along, I settled into my seat with a book, enjoying the peaceful journey.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} 2134
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 2431
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 3214
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 2413
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order is 2431. Explanation: • Sentence 2 starts the paragraph, describing the action of boarding the train early in the morning, just as the sun is rising. • Sentence 4 continues the sequence, explaining how the speaker settled into the seat with a book, enjoying the peaceful journey. • Sentence 3 follows logically, describing the breathtaking scenery outside the train as it moves along. • Sentence 1 concludes the paragraph by describing the arrival of the train at the station, with the speaker stepping onto the platform.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given sentence. A person who avoids social interaction
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Vagabond
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Recluse
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Extrovert
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Optimist
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct one-word substitute for "A person who avoids social interaction" is "Recluse" Explanation: • A recluse is a person who lives a solitary life and avoids social interaction. • A vagabond refers to a person who travels from place to place, often without a permanent home, but doesn't necessarily avoid social interaction. • An extrovert is someone who is outgoing and enjoys socializing, which is the opposite of avoiding social interaction. • An optimist is a person who tends to be hopeful and positive, which is unrelated to avoiding social interaction.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given underlined idiom. When the project failed, the team had to go back to the drawing board.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} To celebrate a small success
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To blame others for the failure
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To start over from the beginning
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To give up completely
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "go back to the drawing board" is "To start over from the beginning" Explanation: • "Go back to the drawing board" means to return to the planning stage or to start a project or process over from the beginning after it has failed or didn't work as expected. • It does not mean to celebrate a small success, blame others, or give up completely. Instead, it refers to reassessing the situation and rethinking the approach.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate Synonym of the bracketed word. The (elusive) criminal managed to evade capture for years.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Clear
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Vivid
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Difficult
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Evade
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct synonym for "Elusive" is "Evade." Explanation: • Elusive means something or someone that is difficult to catch, define, or understand. In the context of the sentence, the criminal is hard to capture, which fits with the meaning of "evade" — to escape or avoid capture. • Clear means easy to understand or see, which is the opposite of "elusive." • Vivid refers to something that is intensely deep or bright, which does not fit the context of something hard to catch. • Difficult refers to something hard to do, but doesn't capture the specific meaning of evading or escaping that "elusive" conveys.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Officially known as the oceanic point of inaccessibility, it's located in the centre of the triangle made by Ducie Island, Motu Nui (part of the Easter Island chain) and Maher Island near Antarctica. B. Point Nemo is the furthest point from land on Earth. C. It is so far from land that the closest humans to Point Nemo are usually astronauts—the International Space Station orbits the Earth from a distance of about 258 miles. D. While the closest inhabited spot to Point Nemo is more than 1670 miles away.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} BACD
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} BADC
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} BCDA
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} BCAD
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order is BACD. Explanation: • B: "Point Nemo is the furthest point from land on Earth." This sentence serves as a strong introduction to the topic. • A: "Officially known as the oceanic point of inaccessibility, it's located in the centre of the triangle made by Ducie Island, Motu Nui (part of the Easter Island chain) and Maher Island near Antarctica." This sentence gives more geographical details, explaining where Point Nemo is located. • C: "It is so far from land that the closest humans to Point Nemo are usually astronauts—the International Space Station orbits the Earth from a distance of about 258 miles." This elaborates on how far Point Nemo is from land and introduces the idea of astronauts being the closest humans. • D: "While the closest inhabited spot to Point Nemo is more than 1670 miles away." This sentence concludes with additional details on the remoteness of Point Nemo.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. She was so tired that she decided to _______ for the night.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Hit the ground running
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Burn the candle at both ends
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Call it a day
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Pull an all-nighter
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is "Call it a day." Explanation: • "Call it a day" means to stop working or stop doing something for the rest of the day, often because you're tired or have finished enough for now. In this context, it fits because the person is so tired that she decides to end her activities for the night. The other options are incorrect because: • "Hit the ground running" means to start something with energy and enthusiasm, which is the opposite of being tired. • "Burn the candle at both ends" refers to staying up late and waking up early, often due to overworking, which doesn't fit the idea of deciding to rest for the night. • "Pull an all-nighter" means staying up all night to work on something, which is also the opposite of deciding to rest.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate Synonym of the given word. Avarice
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Generosity
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Kindness
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Greed
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Charity
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct synonym for "Avarice" is "Greed." Explanation: Avarice refers to an extreme desire for wealth or material gain, often associated with selfishness and insatiability. This is directly synonymous with "Greed", which also means an intense and selfish desire for something, especially wealth.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. The stock market is a complex system where shares of (1)_______ traded companies are traded. It serves as a platform for investors to buy and sell stocks, providing companies with access to capital. The performance of the stock market is (2)_______ seen as an indicator of a country's economic health. When stock prices rise, it is called a bull market, reflecting investor confidence. (3)_________, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall, often due to economic uncertainty or poor corporate performance. Investors use various strategies to make decisions in the stock market. Some rely on fundamental analysis, which involves studying a company's financial statements and market position. Others prefer technical analysis, which focuses on historical price trends and market patterns. Regardless of the approach, the stock market carries (4)______ risks, and investors must be prepared for volatility. Governments and regulatory bodies play a crucial role in ensuring the stock market operates fairly. They enforce rules to prevent insider trading and protect investors from fraud. Additionally, central banks may intervene in the stock market (5)________ adjusting interest rates to stabilize the economy during periods of recession. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Privately
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Publicly
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Secretly
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Illegally
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank 1 is "Publicly." Explanation: The stock market involves the trading of shares of publicly traded companies. A publicly traded company is one whose shares are listed on a stock exchange and available for purchase by the general public.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. The stock market is a complex system where shares of (1)_______ traded companies are traded. It serves as a platform for investors to buy and sell stocks, providing companies with access to capital. The performance of the stock market is (2)_______ seen as an indicator of a country's economic health. When stock prices rise, it is called a bull market, reflecting investor confidence. (3)_________, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall, often due to economic uncertainty or poor corporate performance. Investors use various strategies to make decisions in the stock market. Some rely on fundamental analysis, which involves studying a company's financial statements and market position. Others prefer technical analysis, which focuses on historical price trends and market patterns. Regardless of the approach, the stock market carries (4)______ risks, and investors must be prepared for volatility. Governments and regulatory bodies play a crucial role in ensuring the stock market operates fairly. They enforce rules to prevent insider trading and protect investors from fraud. Additionally, central banks may intervene in the stock market (5)________ adjusting interest rates to stabilize the economy during periods of recession. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Rarely
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Never
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Often
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Occasionally
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in blank 2 is "Often." Explanation: The sentence discusses the stock market's performance as an indicator of a country's economic health. The performance of the stock market is "often" seen as an indicator, meaning it is frequently or commonly regarded in that way.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. The stock market is a complex system where shares of (1)_______ traded companies are traded. It serves as a platform for investors to buy and sell stocks, providing companies with access to capital. The performance of the stock market is (2)_______ seen as an indicator of a country's economic health. When stock prices rise, it is called a bull market, reflecting investor confidence. (3)_________, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall, often due to economic uncertainty or poor corporate performance. Investors use various strategies to make decisions in the stock market. Some rely on fundamental analysis, which involves studying a company's financial statements and market position. Others prefer technical analysis, which focuses on historical price trends and market patterns. Regardless of the approach, the stock market carries (4)______ risks, and investors must be prepared for volatility. Governments and regulatory bodies play a crucial role in ensuring the stock market operates fairly. They enforce rules to prevent insider trading and protect investors from fraud. Additionally, central banks may intervene in the stock market (5)________ adjusting interest rates to stabilize the economy during periods of recession. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Meanwhile
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Therefore
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Conversely
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Similarly
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in blank 3 is "Conversely." Explanation: • "Conversely" means "in contrast" or "oppositely." This fits the sentence because it presents the opposite situation: while a bull market occurs when stock prices rise, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall. So, "Conversely" introduces this contrast. The other options are incorrect because: • "Meanwhile" means "at the same time," which doesn’t fit the context of contrasting two different market conditions. • "Therefore" means "as a result," which implies a cause-and-effect relationship, but there is no such relationship between a bull market and a bear market. • "Similarly" means "in the same way," which would not work here because we're contrasting two different market conditions, not showing similarity.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. The stock market is a complex system where shares of (1)_______ traded companies are traded. It serves as a platform for investors to buy and sell stocks, providing companies with access to capital. The performance of the stock market is (2)_______ seen as an indicator of a country's economic health. When stock prices rise, it is called a bull market, reflecting investor confidence. (3)_________, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall, often due to economic uncertainty or poor corporate performance. Investors use various strategies to make decisions in the stock market. Some rely on fundamental analysis, which involves studying a company's financial statements and market position. Others prefer technical analysis, which focuses on historical price trends and market patterns. Regardless of the approach, the stock market carries (4)______ risks, and investors must be prepared for volatility. Governments and regulatory bodies play a crucial role in ensuring the stock market operates fairly. They enforce rules to prevent insider trading and protect investors from fraud. Additionally, central banks may intervene in the stock market (5)________ adjusting interest rates to stabilize the economy during periods of recession. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Avoidable
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Limited
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Minimal
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Inherent
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in blank 4 is "Inherent." Explanation: • "Inherent" means something that is a permanent and essential part of something. In this context, it refers to the fact that risks are a natural part of the stock market. No matter how much analysis or strategy is used, risk is an inherent (essential and unavoidable) part of investing in the stock market. The other options are incorrect because: • "Avoidable" means something that can be prevented, which is not true in the case of the risks of the stock market — they cannot be completely avoided. • "Limited" means something restricted in extent or quantity, which doesn't fit the context of the stock market, where risks are not necessarily limited. • "Minimal" means very small, but stock market risks are not minimal; they can vary in size and are often significant, depending on various factors.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. The stock market is a complex system where shares of (1)_______ traded companies are traded. It serves as a platform for investors to buy and sell stocks, providing companies with access to capital. The performance of the stock market is (2)_______ seen as an indicator of a country's economic health. When stock prices rise, it is called a bull market, reflecting investor confidence. (3)_________, a bear market occurs when stock prices fall, often due to economic uncertainty or poor corporate performance. Investors use various strategies to make decisions in the stock market. Some rely on fundamental analysis, which involves studying a company's financial statements and market position. Others prefer technical analysis, which focuses on historical price trends and market patterns. Regardless of the approach, the stock market carries (4)______ risks, and investors must be prepared for volatility. Governments and regulatory bodies play a crucial role in ensuring the stock market operates fairly. They enforce rules to prevent insider trading and protect investors from fraud. Additionally, central banks may intervene in the stock market (5)________ adjusting interest rates to stabilize the economy during periods of recession. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} By
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} For
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} With
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Through
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in blank 5 is "by." Explanation: The sentence discusses central banks intervening in the stock market to stabilize the economy during recessions. The correct preposition in this context is "by," which indicates the method or means through which the central banks intervene — in this case, by adjusting interest rates.
